flashcards.back: BFS is a search strategy that explores all nodes at a given depth before moving on to the next depth level.
DFS is a search strategy that explores as far as possible along each branch before backtracking.
UCS is a search strategy that explores the nodes with the lowest cost first.
DLS is a search strategy that explores the nodes up to a certain depth limit.

flashcards.front: What is Iterative Deepening Depth-First Search (IDDFS)?
flashcards.back: IDDFS is a search strategy that combines the benefits of BFS and DFS.
